items.headline: Cupertino faces lawsuit over Mary Avenue safety dispute
items.summary: The City Council discussed ongoing litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns. This case reveals a contested local safety issue significant enough to require legal action.

items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial project is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, one of the largest permits issued in this batch. This represents significant economic development activity in San José's commercial corridor.

items.headline: Palo Alto gas utility rates may increase in next fiscal year
items.summary: The city is considering amendments to gas rate schedules for residential and commercial customers as part of the Fiscal Year 2027 utility financial forecast. Residents should expect potential changes to their utility bills.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and retail incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out a Business License Amnesty Program, outreach initiatives for shopping centers, and economic incentives to attract retail and startups. These programs signal the city's effort to boost local economic development and formalize unlicensed businesses.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes official annual city event
items.summary: San José City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event. The city will now fund the program and accept donations of materials and services.

items.headline: City Council considers retail vitality ordinance for Palo Alto
items.summary: The council is reviewing amendments to zoning and building regulations aimed at implementing retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This could affect how retail spaces are regulated and developed throughout the city.

items.headline: San Antonio Road Area Plan study session underway
items.summary: The city is conducting a land use and transportation alternatives study for the San Antonio Road area, examining how this corridor should develop. This planning effort could influence traffic patterns and land use in that region.

items.headline: New electrical transmission lines coming to wastewater facility
items.summary: LS Power Grid California is installing electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. This infrastructure project affects a major city facility and involves easement agreements for power grid expansion.
